spoiler_note: When spoiler_filtering=true, exclude any entry or detail where spoiler=true. dm_notes fields are always DM-only.

characters.name: Lotan
characters.summary: A pirate-descended Fighter/Warlock hybrid whose warlock pact was hijacked by a mysterious supernatural entity during the dream-contract finalization with a Kraken patron.

details.label: Concept
details.content: Custom Fighter/Warlock hybrid (Eldritch Knight transitioning to Warlock). Descended from a previous Curse of Strahd character.

details.label: Background
details.content: Pirate or pirate-pretender. Arrived in Venturia after sailing from abroad, having met Roxy on the journey.

details.label: Warlock Pact
details.content: Lotan sought patronage from a Kraken. During the dream-contract finalization, a third party hijacked the pact, offering weaker immediate power but greater potential in exchange for help freeing the entity from imprisonment.

details.label: True Patron Identity
details.content: The entity that hijacked Lotan's pact is the Keeper of Faces — the imprisoned Archfey who is one of the campaign's central supernatural forces. Lotan has a direct, personal connection to the primary antagonistic supernatural power of the entire campaign.

details.label: Campaign Role
details.content: His patron connection provides built-in exposition delivery through dreams and visions, personal stakes in the Archfey's imprisonment, moral complexity about whether freeing his patron is good or bad, and a natural channel for plot information.

details.label: Pre-Campaign Connection
details.content: Met Roxy one year before campaign start during her travel to Venturia. Showed her around the city and taught her about merchant areas.

connections.relationship: Met on ship to Venturia; he guided her around the city when they arrived
Patron — an imprisoned Archfey who hijacked Lotan's warlock pact

characters.dm_notes: Lotan's patron IS the Keeper of Faces, the imprisoned Archfey at the center of the campaign. The Keeper can recognize prisoner brands (like Silas's scars) and communicate through dreams. Moral complexity: freeing the Keeper might be necessary but also dangerous. His patron connection is the most direct PC link to the campaign's supernatural core.

characters.name: Caravel "Car" Asteri
characters.summary: A Venturia native and Masquers' Sodality apprentice who is categorically opposed to secrets, having witnessed a friend's death following a secret deal at the Autumn Masquerade.

details.label: Concept
details.content: Custom Masquerade Bard subclass. Born and raised in Venturia.

details.label: Family
details.content: Parents run The Covenant Archive. She has a 'perfect' twin sister favored by her parents. Family are friends with the owners of The Burnt Quill. She rejected the family business.

details.label: Inciting Incident
details.content: Witnessed family friends making a deal with an unknown third party. The next night at the Autumn Masquerade, she found that friend's body dead. Her parents claim the death was caused by 'betrayal' but refuse to elaborate.

details.label: Philosophy
details.content: Categorically opposed to secrets — she blames them for her friend's death. This puts her in direct philosophical conflict with Roxy.

details.label: Career
details.content: Apprentice with the Masquers' Sodality, drawn to music, theater, and masks despite — or perhaps because of — the Masquerade tragedy.

details.label: Connection to Orabella
details.content: The theater star Orabella is infatuated with is the older sister of Car's friend who was killed at the Autumn Masquerade. Car and Orabella have an indirect connection through this performer.

details.label: The Secret Deal
details.content: The 'unknown third party' in the deal Car witnessed may be connected to Silas, the Autumn Masquerade's supernatural significance, or Act One's broader conspiracy. This is a major unresolved thread.

connections.relationship: Unlikely friendship despite opposing philosophies on secrets; Roxy cultivated this friendship partly for access to the Archive
Indirect connection through the theater star Celina Cross, who is the older sister of Car's dead friend
Celina is the older sister of Car's friend who died at the Autumn Masquerade

characters.dm_notes: The 'unknown third party' in the deal Car witnessed is a thread to develop. Her anti-secrets stance creates natural friction with Roxy and thematic resonance with the campaign's hidden truths. Her Masquers' Sodality connection gives access to theater/mask world. Her parents' Archive connection is valuable for investigation hooks.

characters.name: Noname
characters.summary: A Fog Warden Barbarian seeking treatment at Viro's Respite for foggy, unclear memories surrounding her fiancé's recent disappearance.

details.label: Concept
details.content: Barbarian Fog Warden trying to calm her rage. Raised at Liminal House after her parents died young.

details.label: Family Background
details.content: Her father was a 'fog child' — a person who appeared from Vallombrosa with no memories as a child and was raised by Liminal House. Noname herself also went to Liminal House after her parents' early deaths.

details.label: Inciting Incident
details.content: Her fiancé, a fellow Fog Warden, disappeared under mysterious circumstances. Her memory of the event is foggy and unclear. She is currently seeking treatment at Viro's Respite.

details.label: True Cause of Memory Issues
details.content: Silas manipulated Noname's memory during or after the incident where her fiancé disappeared. This was one of his first uses of his memory manipulation abilities in Venturia — crude and incomplete, which is why her memory is 'foggy' rather than cleanly erased.

details.label: Her Fiancé's Fate
details.content: Her fiancé (Marcus, a Fog Warden) disappeared the same night Silas appeared at the Fog Warden post. Marcus is also Kryton's close friend. His ultimate fate — dead, trapped in the prison, transformed, or imprisoned by Silas — is an unresolved campaign question.

connections.relationship: Childhood acquaintance at Liminal House; Orabella is now her healer at Viro's Respite
Both raised at Liminal House; Valentro was present when Noname's fiancé proposed in the Tiered Gardens
Kryton introduced Noname to her fiancé (now disappeared) and feels guilt over the connection
Silas appeared the same night her fiancé vanished and manipulated her memory of the event

characters.dm_notes: Noname sits at the intersection of the Fog Warden disappearances, memory manipulation, and Liminal House themes. Her father being a fog child connects the campaign's deep history to the present. Her foggy memory IS Silas's doing. Consider: Option C (mixed/conflicting memories) for maximum horror. Valentro's father also disappeared as a Fog Warden — the pattern of Fog Wardens being targeted should become apparent.

characters.name: Valentro
characters.summary: An elf Ranger who works in the Tiered Gardens and is haunted by a strange incident where her gardening shears appeared to her as short swords — muscle memory from a past she cannot recall.

details.label: Concept
details.content: Elf Ranger. Works in the Tiered Gardens with her mentor 'Strider' (way of the leaf). Raised at Liminal House after being orphaned.

details.label: Family Loss
details.content: Her father was a Fog Warden who disappeared. Her mother died before her father's disappearance. She was raised at Liminal House.

details.label: The Sword Moment
details.content: While gardening, her shears broke and she suddenly saw them as short swords — muscle memory or a forgotten identity surfacing. This may be evidence of memory bleed, a suppressed warrior past, or reincarnation echoes.

details.label: Roxy's Knowledge of the Sword Moment
details.content: Roxy witnessed the sword incident and is now secretly training Valentro while knowing her secret. Roxy is using this knowledge as leverage while also providing genuine help.

details.label: Roxy's Witness
details.content: Roxy is the only other person who knows about the sword moment. She approached Valentro afterward and offered training — partly genuine, partly to maintain access to a valuable secret.

details.label: True Nature of the Sword Moment
details.content: The sword moment likely represents memory bleed from a suppressed or stolen past identity. Could indicate: reincarnation of one of the original one-shot adventurers, a fog child whose warrior past is surfacing, or a victim of memory manipulation whose true memories are fighting through. Her father's disappearance follows the same Fog Warden targeting pattern as Noname's fiancé.

connections.relationship: Both raised at Liminal House; Valentro was present at Noname's fiancé's proposal in the Tiered Gardens
Both raised at Liminal House, slightly less close than Noname and Orabella
Roxy witnessed the sword incident and is now training Valentro — a relationship with both genuine and strategic dimensions
Kryton may know Valentro casually from frequenting the Tiered Gardens

characters.dm_notes: Valentro's sword moment = memory bleed of same type as the campaign's broader memory manipulation themes. Her father's disappearance as a Fog Warden mirrors Noname's fiancé's disappearance — these two should realize the pattern together. Roxy's training relationship is both genuine and strategic. Potential reincarnation hook for later campaign development.

characters.name: Kryton Novelli
characters.summary: A VAVA faculty member and former Wizard turned Warlock whose memories — including his wizard spell knowledge — were stolen. Bound to an unknown patron through a desperate ritual he believes he can control.

details.label: Concept
details.content: Mid-career VAVA faculty member, brilliant but increasingly erratic. Former Wizard who is now a Warlock. Has a gambling problem at The Undertow.

details.label: The Disappearance
details.content: His close friend — a Fog Warden he introduced to Noname — disappeared the same night Silas appeared. Kryton investigated obsessively and discovered patterns in Fog Warden disappearances and evidence of city-wide memory manipulation.

details.label: Memory Theft
details.content: Someone working through The Undertow stole Kryton's memories, including his wizard spell knowledge. He lost his magical abilities and much of his investigative findings.

details.label: The Warlock Pact
details.content: Desperate to recover his abilities, Kryton gambled at The Undertow trying to win his memories back. Instead he won someone else's memory — a vision of a warlock tome and ritual. He performed the ritual and bound himself to an unknown patron.

details.label: His Fatal Flaw
details.content: Kryton believes he can outsmart his patron and find loopholes in the contract. He doesn't understand that his patron is manipulating him toward something, that the pact is far more binding than he read, and that her agenda may unleash catastrophe.

details.label: True Patron Identity
details.content: Kryton's patron is Tartuzi the sphinx — the same sphinx whose consultation with Lorenzo centuries ago led directly to her own enslavement as the Archfey's prison lock. She has been bound for a millennium as the eternal lock of the imprisonment mechanism. Her agenda, what she wants Kryton to do, and whether freeing her is a good outcome are all major unresolved questions.

connections.relationship: Introduced Noname to her fiancé (the disappeared Fog Warden); feels profound guilt
Kryton's investigation led him to discover patterns Silas is responsible for; his memories were likely stolen to suppress this knowledge
His unknown warlock patron — a sphinx imprisoned for a millennium as the lock of the Keeper's prison
Regular gambling patron; the location where his memories were stolen and where he won the warlock ritual

characters.dm_notes: Kryton's patron IS Tartuzi. This creates a fascinating dynamic: his patron is simultaneously a victim (imprisoned for a millennium) and potentially dangerous (her agenda is unknown). His intellectual arrogance is the perfect tragic flaw — he thinks he's outsmarting her but she's been playing this game for a thousand years. His warlock levels function normally; his wizard levels are inaccessible (memories stolen). The memory theft happened through The Undertow connection — explore who or what orchestrates memory theft there.

characters.name: Orabella
characters.summary: A fog child raised at Liminal House who now works as a cleric at St. Viro's Respite. She experiences vivid first-person dreams of being other people — dreams she believes are memories from lives she has lived.

details.label: Concept
details.content: Cleric working at St. Viro's Respite. Fog child who appeared at Liminal House as a child with no memory of her life before.

details.label: The Dreams
details.content: Shortly after arriving at Liminal House, Orabella began having vivid first-person dreams where she IS someone else — different body, different life, different circumstances. Always first-person perspective. Always feeling real in a way normal dreams don't. The first dream: blood all over her hands and clothes. She doesn't know whose blood or why.

details.label: Her Theory
details.content: Orabella believes these dreams mean she has been inside Vallombrosa — not just appeared from it, but genuinely been inside the fog. She believes they are memories of lives she has lived. The institutions refuse to discuss it with her.

details.label: Institutional Silence
details.content: As she aged and started asking about the dreams, Liminal House staff and Viro's Respite clergy shut her down. No one will discuss what the dreams mean or whether other fog children experience them. This has made her more determined to find answers.

details.label: Current Patients
details.content: Orabella is caring for two critical patients: Noname (childhood acquaintance, foggy memories of fiancé's disappearance) and Silas (mysterious scarred comatose man who appeared the same night Noname's fiancé vanished).

details.label: Theater Obsession
details.content: Orabella is deeply interested in theater and infatuated with a particular star performer — Celina Cross. This fascination mirrors her dream experience: actors temporarily become someone else, just as she inhabits other identities in dreams.

details.label: Investigation
details.content: Orabella is attempting to access hidden records kept by Viro's Respite and Liminal House to understand what her dreams mean, whether other fog children experience this, and why the institutions suppressed her questions.

details.label: True Nature of the Dreams
details.content: Orabella's recurring first-person dreams are connected to the campaign's reincarnation mechanics. She is likely a reincarnation of one of the original adventurers from the Sphinx Twice one-shot who were imprisoned in the di Errante dungeon. Her first dream (blood on hands) may be her death or the final battle. The fog preserves or recycles souls, and fog children may be souls being reborn through the liminal space between lives.

details.label: Institutional Conspiracy
details.content: Liminal House and Viro's Respite know that other fog children experience these dreams. There is a deliberate policy of non-disclosure. They may be protecting fog children from dangerous knowledge OR hiding something about Vallombrosa's true nature — possibly both.

connections.relationship: Childhood acquaintance at Liminal House; now Noname's healer at Viro's Respite — both experiencing different forms of memory disruption
Both raised at Liminal House, slightly less close; potential reconnection through Noname
Indirect connection through Celina Cross — the theater star is the older sister of Car's friend who died at the Autumn Masquerade
Orabella is infatuated with this theater performer; attends her shows whenever possible
Orabella is his caretaker while he is comatose; she is studying his unusual scars

characters.dm_notes: Orabella has TOO MANY identities (opposite of other memory victims). Her struggle: which memories are actually hers? She sits at the exact center of the memory crisis — caring for Silas (memory manipulator) and Noname (memory victim). Her institutional investigation is a major early plot thread. Celina Cross connection bridges her to Car. Possible reincarnation of one-shot adventurer — handle carefully so it creates meaningful moments rather than feeling like a plot twist for its own sake.

characters.name: Roxanya
characters.summary: A 19-year-old forest gnome raised by a community of Brownies who genuinely believed she was one of them. Now a VAVA student and secret collector driven by a divine pact with Garl Glittergold and a mission to save enslaved Brownies.

details.label: Concept
details.content: Arcane Trickster Rogue. 3ft tall, sun-tanned skin with freckles, wild curly red hair. Always has a taxidermy owl on her shoulder.

details.label: Origin
details.content: Abandoned as an infant in the forests of Aminon outside Hearthspire. Found by a community of Brownies (called a 'rummage'). Until age 8-9, genuinely believed she was just an unusually large Brownie. At age 12-13, trading in nearby cities, she learned she was a gnome — her second identity crisis.

details.label: The Mission
details.content: At age 12, a masked stranger from Venturia told her about Vallombrosa. She learned that Brownies are being enslaved and taken to the cursed valley. At 14, she formulated a plan: attend VAVA, learn powerful magic, save the Brownies. She arrived at 18 with the rummage's blessing.

details.label: Meeting Lotan
details.content: On the ship to Venturia, she met Lotan and was enamored by his pirate stories. She adopted swashbuckler attire. He showed her around Venturia when they arrived and taught her about merchant areas.

details.label: Divine Pact with Garl Glittergold
details.content: Upon arriving in Venturia, she immediately tried to rob the Penny Shrine — and Garl Glittergold himself appeared, amused by her audacity. He offered divine patronage in exchange for occasional favors, granting her Arcane Trickster powers and making her obsessed with Vallombrosa, secrets, and hidden knowledge.

details.label: Strategic Relationships
details.content: Roxy's friendships have strategic dimensions she doesn't always disclose. She befriended Car specifically for access to the Archive and mask guilds. She is training Valentro while also holding her sword-moment secret as leverage. Her relationship with Lotan may be her most genuine, but even that has layers.

details.label: VAVA Facade
details.content: Roxy barely passed her VAVA interview ('by some miracle'). She is enrolled focusing on illusion magic, but her powers are divine gifts from Garl, not the result of study. She maintains a facade of magical competence.

details.label: Nightly Escape
details.content: She cannot sleep in her VAVA dorm — too confining after 18 years outdoors. She sneaks out nightly to sleep in the Tiered Gardens, which is how she witnessed Valentro's sword incident.

details.label: The Masked Stranger
details.content: The masked person who told 12-year-old Roxy about Venturia and Vallombrosa is an unresolved mystery. Was this deliberate recruitment? A Masquers' Sodality member? Someone connected to the main plot? Did they know about the Brownie enslavement connection?

connections.relationship: Met on ship to Venturia; genuine friendship (or as genuine as Roxy's friendships get); he was her first guide to the city
Unlikely friendship; Roxy cultivated this partly for Archive/guild access despite their opposing philosophies on secrets
Witnessed Valentro's sword incident; now training her with strategic and genuine dimensions both
Divine patron who granted her Arcane Trickster abilities and obsession with secrets/Vallombrosa
Possibly her VAVA teacher; may or may not know about her true situation

characters.dm_notes: Roxy is living a lie at every level — not a Brownie, not a proper gnome, not a real mage (divine gifts), not fully honest with her friends. Her mission (saving Brownies from Vallombrosa) is a genuine emotional driver but also means she's using everyone around her as tools. The moment her strategic relationships are exposed will be a major character crisis. Garl's true agenda remains unclear — what favors will he eventually ask that force her to choose? The masked stranger who told her about Venturia at age 12 is deliberately unresolved — could connect to major plot threads.

characters.name: Silas
characters.summary: A mysterious comatose man covered in unusual scars who appeared at the Fog Warden post the same night Noname's fiancé disappeared. He is currently being treated at St. Viro's Respite under Orabella's care.

details.label: Current State
details.content: Comatose at St. Viro's Respite. His body is covered in strange scars in unusual patterns. Orabella is his caretaker and has been studying the scars. He occasionally makes sounds suggesting consciousness.

details.label: The Scars
details.content: The scars on his body are not natural wounds. They are supernatural brands burned into his flesh — patterns that radiate faint magical energy upon close examination.

details.label: His True Identity
details.content: Silas is a scholar who was previously imprisoned — bearing the Archfey's prisoner brands marking him as property of the Keeper of Faces. He escaped from connection to the weakening prison. His coma is magically induced, not natural.

details.label: What He Is Capable Of
details.content: Silas has memory manipulation abilities connected to the Bound mechanics. He manipulated Noname's memory during or after the Fog Warden incident as one of his first crude uses of this power in Venturia. When he wakes, he will be capable of creating Bound individuals and more sophisticated memory manipulation.

details.label: The Prisoner Brands
details.content: The scars are the Archfey's prisoner brands — Lotan's patron (the Keeper of Faces) would recognize these if shown or described. This is a critical early investigation hook that connects Silas to the campaign's supernatural core.

details.label: When He Wakes
details.content: Recommended awakening window: Sessions 3-5. This gives time for investigation, allows Orabella to develop a patient relationship, and makes his waking a major event rather than an immediate confrontation.

connections.relationship: Orabella is his caretaker at Viro's Respite; she is studying his scars and may be affected by his presence
He manipulated Noname's memory of the night her fiancé disappeared; appeared at the same location
Bears the Keeper's prisoner brands; his abilities derive from connection to the weakening prison

characters.dm_notes: Silas's secondary agenda beyond scholarly research is still open. What specifically is he building toward that climaxes at the Masquerade? Does he create all Bound individuals, or primarily targeted ones? His patient care relationship with Orabella is rich territory — she might begin to sense something wrong before he wakes. Lotan's patron can identify the prisoner brands if the party describes the scars to him. The Bound mechanics: supernatural compulsion, not free agreement — disturbs the Covenant of Silver Scales.

characters.name: Celina Cross
characters.summary: A celebrated performer at the Amaranth Theater. Orabella is infatuated with her and attends her shows whenever possible. She is the older sister of Car's friend who died at the Autumn Masquerade.

details.label: Public Identity
details.content: A star performer at the Amaranth Theater in the High Quarter. One of the most celebrated actors on the island. Orabella attends her performances regularly.

details.label: Family Connection
details.content: She is the older sister of the friend Car witnessed making a secret deal before dying at the Autumn Masquerade. She may or may not know the full circumstances of her sibling's death.

details.label: True Supernatural Significance
details.content: Celina Cross is the reincarnation of Isabella Fortuna — the woman at the center of the original di Errante tragedy. She is the one Marcello will seek when he eventually arrives in Venturia. She must be developed as a complete, compelling person before this significance is revealed — players need to care about her as an individual first.

details.label: Marcello's Target
details.content: When Marcello arrives (Phase Two), he will pursue Celina in a supernatural courtship driven by his ancient obsession. Protecting her from his dream manipulation and memory implantation will become a central campaign challenge. She should have agency in her own defense, not be purely a prize to protect.

connections.relationship: Orabella is her devoted theater fan; the infatuation mirrors Orabella's dream experience of inhabiting other identities
Celina's sibling was Car's friend who died at the Autumn Masquerade
Marcello will pursue Celina in Phase Two — she is the reincarnation of Isabella whom his ancient obsession centers on

characters.dm_notes: CRITICAL: Develop Celina as a fully realized person before any plot significance is revealed. Orabella's infatuation should grow into genuine connection. Players need to love her as a character. The theater/performance resonance with Orabella's dreams (both about inhabiting other identities) is thematically rich. Does she recognize Orabella as a devoted fan? Have they met? Does she suspect foul play in her sibling's death?

characters.name: The Keeper of Faces
characters.summary: An Archfey whose domain centers on masks, identity, reflection, and glamour. Currently imprisoned after being trapped by Lorenzo di Errante's scheme centuries ago. Acts as Lotan's warlock patron.

details.label: Domain
details.content: The Keeper of Faces' domain centers on masks, identity, reflection, and glamour. Grants bargains involving reputation, appearance, social standing, identity, and recognition.

details.label: Imprisonment
details.content: Lorenzo di Errante orchestrated the Keeper's imprisonment by manipulating an adventuring party into performing a binding ritual. Trapped at the moment of legitimate debt collection, the Keeper was drained of power by Lorenzo. Tartuzi the sphinx was pulled into the mechanism as the eternal lock of the prison.

details.label: Lotan's Connection
details.content: The Keeper hijacked Lotan's warlock pact with a Kraken, offering weaker immediate power but greater potential if Lotan helps free it from imprisonment. Communicates through dreams and visions.

details.label: Recognizing Prisoner Brands
details.content: The Keeper would recognize Silas's scars as its own prisoner brands if Lotan describes or shows them. This is a critical early investigation hook.

connections.relationship: Warlock patron — hijacked Lotan's pact and communicates through dreams and visions
Silas bears the Keeper's prisoner brands; his abilities derive from connection to the weakening prison
Tartuzi is the eternal lock of the prison that holds the Keeper

characters.dm_notes: The Keeper is the campaign's central supernatural force. Its imprisonment is directly connected to Lorenzo's theft and Tartuzi's enslavement. Freeing it may be necessary but carries unknown consequences. The Keeper is a victim, but also potentially dangerous. Moral complexity around whether freeing it is good or bad should be maintained throughout.

characters.name: Marcello di Errante
characters.summary: The di Errante heir who will arrive in Venturia in Phase Two, claiming the ancient family estates. His reality-warping abilities and supernatural courtship of Celina Cross make him the campaign's primary antagonist.

details.label: True Identity Question
details.content: It is unclear — and may remain deliberately ambiguous — whether the arriving Marcello is the original Marcello (the elder brother, beloved and accomplished) or Lorenzo using his stolen power to take his brother's face and identity. Lorenzo's bargain with the Keeper of Faces resulted in him appearing as Marcello and being unable to speak his own name.

details.label: The Original Marcello
details.content: Marcello Prime was the elder brother — powerful with fey-touched nature, particularly shadow manipulation. He was respected, accomplished, and genuinely beloved. Isabella truly loved him freely and completely.

details.label: Lorenzo's Bargain
details.content: Lorenzo, driven by jealousy and entitlement, made a bargain with the Keeper of Faces to coerce Isabella's love. The price was 'the face you show the world' — collected seven years later as his literal identity, name, and sense of self. When the power sustaining Isabella's coercion collapsed, she confronted Lorenzo; he murdered her, then killed Marcello. Tartuzi appeared and became the eternal prison lock.

details.label: The Arriving Figure
details.content: The Marcello who arrives is genuinely monstrous — villainy from jealousy and entitlement rather than tragic love. He will pursue Celina through supernatural courtship, manipulate city politics, and exploit Venturia's fractured council structure.

details.label: Council Infiltration Strategy
details.content: Marcello doesn't need to control the gridlocked council — he charms Harbor-Lords with economic solutions, exploits Guild-Master divisions, manipulates the Temple Authority through false friendship, and makes a deal with the Watch-Captain. By the time the council recognizes the danger, he is already established.

connections.relationship: Marcello will pursue Celina — she is Isabella's reincarnation and the object of his ancient obsession
Lorenzo made the original bargain with the Keeper, leading to the Keeper's imprisonment and Lorenzo's identity theft
Tartuzi's consultation with Lorenzo led directly to her own imprisonment as the eternal prison lock

characters.dm_notes: Whether arriving Marcello is original Marcello or Lorenzo should remain deliberately ambiguous for maximum narrative impact. He is genuinely monstrous, not tragically sympathetic. Villainy from jealousy and entitlement. His arrival triggers Phase Two. The party needs investment in Celina before he arrives. His infiltration of the council uses existing gridlock — he doesn't need to control it, just benefit from the chaos.

characters.name: Tartuzi
characters.summary: A sphinx who consulted with Lorenzo di Errante centuries ago, unknowingly providing the information he used to imprison the Keeper of Faces. She was pulled into the imprisonment mechanism itself and has been its eternal lock for a millennium.

details.label: Her Role in the Tragedy
details.content: Tartuzi provided Lorenzo with the precise binding date and the principles for binding an Archfey — information a sphinx must give freely when asked. Lorenzo used this consultation to orchestrate the Keeper's imprisonment. At the moment of catastrophe, Tartuzi was pulled by the completed binding mechanism to become the eternal lock of the prison. Her consultation, given freely according to sphinx nature, led directly to her own millennia of enslavement.

details.label: Current State
details.content: Tartuzi has been the eternal lock of the Keeper's prison for approximately a thousand years. She is both prisoner and prison mechanism. Her consciousness persists, her agenda unknown.

details.label: Kryton's Patron
details.content: Tartuzi is Kryton's unknown warlock patron. The memory Kryton 'won' at The Undertow — a vision of a warlock tome and ritual — was arranged so he would find it. She has been manipulating events to bind herself to a champion, presumably toward some goal connected to her imprisonment or freedom.

details.label: Open Questions
details.content: Does Tartuzi want Marcello dead, alive, or is she indifferent? Does she survive into Act Two? If freed, what role does she play in the endgame? Is freeing her from a millennium of servitude a goal the party should pursue?

connections.relationship: Kryton's unknown warlock patron; she arranged events to bind him to her service
She is the eternal lock of the Keeper's prison — bound to the mechanism for a thousand years
Lorenzo's consultation with her led to her imprisonment; her feelings about Marcello (original or Lorenzo) are unknown

characters.dm_notes: Tartuzi is simultaneously a victim (tricked into a millennium of enslavement) and potentially dangerous (her agenda unknown, she has been manipulating Kryton). Her nature as a sphinx means she gave Lorenzo information freely — she couldn't refuse. This makes her tragic rather than culpable. Major open question: does freeing her resolve the imprisonment crisis or create a new one? Kryton's intellectual arrogance that he can outsmart her is the irony — she's been playing this game for a thousand years.

locations.name: Venturian Academy of Veiled Arts
locations.summary: The foremost institution of magical study on Seravalle, rising from the High Quarter with autumn-colored spires and candlelit windows. Its distinctive amber-and-charcoal robes mark alumni throughout Venturian society.

details.label: Location
details.content: High Quarter. Tagline: 'Where knowledge wears many masks.'

details.label: Description
details.content: The Academy sprawls across several interconnected buildings, each devoted to different disciplines. The Veiled Tower houses divination faculty. The Binding Hall teaches contract magic and oath-craft. The Glamour Courts train illusionists in spaces where the boundaries between appearance and reality have always been negotiable.

details.label: The Library
details.content: Five floors of collected knowledge, with the deepest levels requiring special permission and, some say, a willingness to forget certain things you might read there.

details.label: The Veil Archive
details.content: VAVA maintains a Veil Archive — a restricted collection containing two centuries of historical research into supernatural phenomena now officially prohibited for study. The Archmagister Dorian holds the key. Scholars who want access face political and institutional barriers.

details.label: PC Connections
details.content: Kryton is a faculty member. Roxy is a first-year student maintaining a facade of magical competence. Car may have connections through family.

connections.relationship: Faculty member — brilliant but increasingly erratic
First-year student; her powers are divine gifts, not academic achievement

locations.dm_notes: The Veil Archive is a critical resource for later investigation. Archmagister Dorian faces impossible choices about opening it. The prohibition on research into certain phenomena (likely Vallombrosa-adjacent topics) creates tension between institutional caution and the party's need for information.

locations.name: St. Viro's Respite
locations.summary: The primary healing institution in Venturia, operated by the Abbey of St. Viro. Orabella works here as a cleric. Currently housing both Noname (for memory treatment) and Silas (comatose, unknown identity).

details.label: Function
details.content: Venturia's primary medical and healing facility. Operated in coordination with St. Viro's Abbey. Staff include clerics, healers, and scholars of medical and spiritual care.

details.label: Current Notable Patients
details.content: Noname is receiving treatment for foggy memories surrounding her fiancé's disappearance. Silas — the mysterious comatose man with unusual scars — is housed here under Orabella's care.

details.label: Hidden Records
details.content: Viro's Respite maintains hidden records that Orabella is trying to access. These records document fog children, their experiences, and information the institution refuses to share publicly. The records likely contain documentation of other fog children with dreams similar to Orabella's, historical accounts of similar cases, and theories the staff have suppressed.

details.label: Institutional Silence on Fog Children
details.content: The Respite's clergy actively deflect questions from fog children about their experiences and dreams. This is a deliberate policy of non-disclosure. Whether this protects fog children from dangerous knowledge or hides something the institution wants buried is an open question.

connections.relationship: Orabella works here as a cleric and caretaker to both Noname and Silas
Patient receiving treatment for memory issues
Comatose patient of unknown identity; Orabella is studying his unusual scars
Closely coordinated institutions; both operated under St. Viro's Abbey's oversight

locations.dm_notes: Orabella's quest to access hidden records is a major early plot thread. The records almost certainly contain information that would help the party — and information that someone doesn't want discovered. The institutional silence is likely a combination of genuine protection and politically motivated suppression.

locations.name: Liminal House
locations.summary: An institution in the Market Tiers that specializes in housing and caring for 'fog children' — people who appear from Vallombrosa with no memories of their lives before. Noname, Valentro, and Orabella all grew up here.

details.label: History
details.content: Founded approximately 1,100 years ago by Brother Aldric, a scholar-monk who recognized that fog children needed specialized care. It predates most of Venturia's other institutions and has been dealing with fog children since the first ones appeared three weeks after the di Errante tragedy.

details.label: Operating Principles
details.content: Don't ask children about their pasts. Don't try to identify families. Don't record circumstances of arrival. Teach practical skills for building futures. Foster community and family bonds between residents. Maintain close coordination with St. Viro's Abbey.

details.label: Physical Layout
details.content: Sprawls across three connected buildings: The Old Wing (administrative, director's quarters, Archive), The Residence Hall (housing 30-40 children), and The Workshop Wing (classrooms, training spaces, kitchen, common room). Architecture feels maze-like to outsiders but logical to residents.

details.label: What the Institution Actually Knows
details.content: What Aldric never wrote down but successive directors have quietly understood: the children who arrive are connected to Vallombrosa. Some are children who wandered into the fog decades or centuries ago and emerged unchanged while time passed outside. Others may be born inside Vallombrosa and expelled or escaped. The institution knows far more than it tells residents.

details.label: The Archive
details.content: The Old Wing contains an unofficial archive — ostensibly records of current residents, but actually documentation of strange patterns in arrivals going back centuries. This is distinct from the hidden medical records at Viro's Respite and likely contains even more historically significant information.

connections.relationship: Noname was raised here after her parents died; her father was himself a fog child raised here
Valentro was raised here after being orphaned
Orabella appeared from Vallombrosa as a fog child and was raised here; began experiencing her dreams shortly after arrival
Sister institution under St. Viro's Abbey oversight; shared resources and coordinated responses

locations.dm_notes: The Liminal House archive is a goldmine for late Act One/Act Two investigation. It contains centuries of fog child records. Three PCs grew up here — this institution is deeply personal stakes territory. The pattern of fog child arrivals likely maps to historical events inside Vallombrosa. Noname's father being a fog child means she may have inherited some connection to the fog beyond just growing up here.

locations.name: The Tiered Gardens
locations.summary: An elaborate public garden space where Valentro works as a groundskeeper under her mentor Strider. The site of Noname's fiancé's proposal and Valentro's mysterious sword incident, which Roxy witnessed.

details.label: Function
details.content: Public garden maintained by professional groundskeepers. Known as a place for contemplation and peaceful retreat. Kryton frequents it. Used for significant personal moments — Noname's fiancé proposed here.

details.label: PC Significance
details.content: Valentro works here under her mentor 'Strider.' Noname's fiancé proposed to her here, with Valentro present. Roxy sneaks here nightly to sleep (she can't sleep indoors after 18 years outside). The sword incident occurred here, witnessed by Roxy.

connections.relationship: Works here as a groundskeeper; site of the sword incident
Sleeps here nightly; witnessed Valentro's sword incident
Her fiancé proposed to her in these gardens

locations.dm_notes: A gentle, personal location that contrasts with the supernatural dread elsewhere. Good for character development scenes. The proposal memory is bittersweet — Valentro was there, Noname remembers it (or should), and the fiancé is now gone.

locations.name: The Fog Wardens' Garrison
locations.summary: A stone building and watchtower at the Overlook where Venturia ends and Vallombrosa's fog begins. Fog Wardens maintain the barriers, record those approaching the fog line, and document unusual events.

details.label: Location
details.content: The Overlook — where the city ends and Vallombrosa's fog begins. A small garrison with watchtower that has watched the fog boundary for generations.

details.label: The Logs
details.content: The garrison's logs make for unsettling reading: lights moving in the mist, voices in unrecognized languages, figures visible just inside the fog boundary standing motionless before fading, measurements showing the fog line has moved by inches or feet over years.

details.label: Effects of Long Service
details.content: Most rotations last only a few months. Longer exposure produces sleep difficulties, persistent feelings of being watched, and dreams about places the dreamer has never been. Some guards request extensions anyway, claiming they need to understand something they almost glimpsed.

details.label: The Disappearance Site
details.content: Noname's fiancé (Marcus) disappeared from his post at the garrison the same night Silas appeared there. This is the primary crime scene for the opening investigation. Other Fog Wardens may have witnessed something — or had their memories affected.

details.label: Targeting Pattern
details.content: The pattern of Fog Warden disappearances — Valentro's father earlier, Noname's fiancé most recently — suggests Vallombrosa is 'leaking' before Marcello's arrival, pulling people with knowledge of the boundary into the fog. Fog Wardens specifically may know something that makes them targets, or their proximity to the boundary makes them vulnerable.

connections.relationship: Her fiancé disappeared from this post; she may have incomplete memories of the night
Valentro's father disappeared from Fog Warden duty earlier — same pattern
Silas appeared at this location the same night Marcus disappeared

locations.dm_notes: The garrison logs are an investigation resource — players can find records there. The fog line movement measurements are subtle evidence of the prison weakening. Other Fog Wardens who were on duty that night are key witnesses — some may have had partial memory manipulation.

locations.name: The Amaranth Theater
locations.summary: The cultural heart of Venturia, a grand performance complex of deep red stone and gilded trim in the High Quarter. For over three centuries it has staged everything from intimate performances to the annual Tragedy of Vallombrosa during the Autumn Masquerade.

details.label: Description
details.content: Dominates the upper edge of the High Quarter, visible from half the city. The main house seats over a thousand with acoustically perfect staging — whispers from the stage reach back rows with crystalline clarity. Smaller venues throughout the complex host experimental works.

details.label: The Tragedy of Vallombrosa
details.content: The Amaranth's crown jewel is its annual staging of 'The Tragedy of Vallombrosa' during the Autumn Masquerade — the most anticipated cultural event on the island. Each year brings a different director's interpretation. The city debates productions for months.

details.label: Strange Phenomena
details.content: Performers say the theater feels different during Masquerade season — more alive, more demanding. Veterans learn not to look too long into dressing room mirrors during the Tragedy's run. Understudies sometimes get their chance to perform through circumstances no one can explain afterward.

details.label: PC Connections
details.content: Orabella attends performances here regularly, drawn to Celina Cross. Car is a Masquers' Sodality apprentice with professional connections to the theater world. The Autumn Masquerade where Car's friend died was held here.

connections.relationship: Celina is a star performer of the resident company
Orabella attends Celina's performances here whenever possible
Masquers' Sodality apprentice with theater connections; her friend died at the Masquerade held here

locations.dm_notes: The Autumn Masquerade at the Amaranth is a major calendar event — use it as a deadline or climax point. The dressing room mirrors and understudies-getting-chances are hooks for supernatural investigation. Was Orabella present at the Masquerade when Car's friend died?

locations.name: The Undertow
locations.summary: A gambling establishment in the Harbor District. Kryton has a gambling problem here. The location where his memories were stolen and where he later won the vision of a warlock ritual.

details.label: Function
details.content: Gambling establishment in the Harbor District. Well-known to Kryton and others who frequent it regularly.

details.label: Kryton's History Here
details.content: Kryton's gambling problem has deep roots at The Undertow. Someone working through this establishment stole his memories — including his wizard spell knowledge. When he returned desperate to recover them, he instead won 'someone else's memory' — a vision of a warlock tome and ritual.

details.label: Supernatural Significance
details.content: The Undertow appears to be a venue where memories can be traded, stolen, or won. The mechanism for this — whether it is a deliberate supernatural operation or an emergent property of the establishment — is unknown. It is likely connected to the broader memory manipulation occurring in Venturia.

details.label: Political Connection
details.content: Constanza Marea (a Harbor-Lord council member) has been meeting secretly with The Undertow's representatives. She either owes them something significant or is working a deal that would horrify the rest of the council.

connections.relationship: Regular patron with a gambling problem; site of his memory theft and warlock ritual discovery
She has been meeting secretly with Undertow representatives — leverage or obligation unknown

locations.dm_notes: The Undertow is almost certainly a hub for the memory manipulation network. Who runs it and how the memory trading works are investigative threads. The Constanza connection gives this political weight. Kryton winning the 'wrong' memory there may have been arranged — someone wanted him to find that ritual and bind himself to Tartuzi.

locations.name: The Penny Shrine
locations.summary: A modest shrine wedged between warehouses in the Harbor District, layered with centuries of offerings from sailors, travelers, and desperate people. Has no official deity, priesthood, or doctrine — just hope.

details.label: Description
details.content: A single room layered with accumulated offerings: coins from every trading nation, found objects, written prayers in dozens of languages, candles never all allowed to go out at once. The keeper maintains the space without making claims about whether prayers are heard.

details.label: Peculiarities
details.content: Offerings sometimes rearrange overnight into meaningful patterns. Certain prayers seem to be answered more reliably than others — nothing dramatic, but enough to sustain hope. Candles sometimes flicker in unison, as if responding to something visitors cannot perceive.

details.label: Roxy's Origin Story
details.content: Roxy immediately tried to rob the Penny Shrine upon arriving in Venturia. Garl Glittergold himself appeared, amused by her audacity, and offered her the divine pact that granted her Arcane Trickster powers.

connections.relationship: Site of her attempted robbery and subsequent divine pact with Garl Glittergold

locations.dm_notes: A warm, character-meaningful location. The candle behaviors and offering rearrangements could be subtle divine intervention or just the shrine's accumulated supernatural charge from centuries of sincere prayer.

locations.name: The Ferriers' Confraternity
locations.summary: Venturia's ancient guild of gondola ferriers, reachable only by water. They know every canal in the city including ones that don't appear on official maps, and maintain traditions outsiders find peculiar but never quite explain.

details.label: Location
details.content: A converted boathouse in a quiet backwater where three canals meet — a junction that doesn't appear on any official map. Visitors arriving by foot find only blank walls and locked gates. Those arriving by gondola find an arched entrance onto lantern-lit docks.

details.label: Traditions
details.content: Ferriers do not speak while passing under bridges — those who break the silence report voices echoing strangely, as though something beneath the water was listening. Payment always goes in a bronze dish, never handed directly. Most significantly: the Confraternity refuses to ferry passengers after midnight during the week before the Autumn Masquerade. No explanation given.

details.label: Ancient Knowledge
details.content: The Confraternity predates the curse. Their oldest records claim they were ferrying passengers when the di Errante estates still stood. Masters know waterways that shouldn't exist.

details.label: What They Know
details.content: The pre-Masquerade embargo on midnight ferrying suggests the Confraternity understands something supernatural about those nights. Their refusal and the reports of canals shifting and folding until dawn suggest the waterways become genuinely dangerous — possibly connected to Vallombrosa's cycles or the Archfey's imprisonment mechanics.

locations.dm_notes: The Confraternity is a slow-burn information source. Masters who know canals that don't exist on maps could be guides to unreachable locations. Their pre-Masquerade behavior is a clue about supernatural cycles. The 'something beneath the water listening' under bridges is an intriguing unexplained element.

locations.name: The Vellum Hall
locations.summary: Venturia's public library in the Market Tiers, open to any citizen. Covers navigation, medicine, law, trade, languages, and more. Contains a Restricted Stacks section that is the city's open secret.

details.label: Description
details.content: A converted merchant hall with good light and sturdy shelving. Distinguished from VAVA's exclusive archives by being genuinely accessible to working people. Librarians have spent generations collecting practical knowledge.

details.label: The Restricted Stacks
details.content: Every book ever banned in Venturia is supposedly preserved here, accessible only to those on the Head Librarian's waiting list — which is long, slow-moving, and determined by criteria no one has articulated. Applicants wait years. Some die still waiting.

locations.dm_notes: The Restricted Stacks waiting list criteria could be a puzzle — what does the Head Librarian actually look for? Knowing the right things, approaching correctly, or having the right connections could bypass years of waiting.

factions.name: The Liminal House
factions.summary: A specialized institution caring for 'fog children' — people who appear from Vallombrosa with no memories. Founded over a thousand years ago, it houses 30-40 children at any time and is closely coordinated with St. Viro's Abbey.

details.label: Public Function
details.content: Provides housing, education, community, and practical skills to fog children who arrive in Venturia with no memory of their lives before. Funded by St. Viro's Abbey, Council grants, alumni donations, and trade income from older residents.

details.label: Known Policies
details.content: No investigation of origins. No records of arrival circumstances. Focus on building futures, not recovering histories. Regular coordination with St. Viro's Abbey.

details.label: What They Actually Know
details.content: Successive directors have quietly understood that fog children are connected to Vallombrosa — some emerged from the fog decades or centuries ago and are unchanged while time passed outside, others may be born inside Vallombrosa. The House maintains a hidden archive documenting patterns in arrivals. They are deliberately suppressing this information from residents.

details.label: Reasons for Suppression
details.content: Possible reasons for institutional silence include: protecting fog children from traumatic knowledge about their own nature, concealing the depth of Vallombrosa's supernatural influence, orders from a higher power (the Abbey, the Watch, or someone older), or a supernatural mechanism that prevents disclosure.

connections.relationship: Sister institution, closely coordinated, both under Abbey oversight
Former resident; now trying to access their hidden records
Former resident; her father was also a fog child raised here
Former resident

factions.dm_notes: The hidden archive is a critical late Act One investigation target. The patterns in fog child arrivals likely map to historical events inside Vallombrosa. Three PCs have deep personal stakes here. Noname's father being a fog child suggests the phenomenon has been recurring for generations — possibly connected to the prison weakening in cycles.

factions.name: The Masquers' Sodality
factions.summary: The professional guild overseeing Venturia's theatrical and masquerade traditions. Car is an apprentice here, drawn to music, theater, and masks despite — or because of — the Masquerade tragedy in her past.

details.label: Function
details.content: Governs theatrical performance, mask-making, and masquerade traditions throughout Venturia. Apprentices train in performance, craft, and the cultural traditions surrounding the Autumn Masquerade. Closely connected to the Amaranth Theater.

details.label: Significance in the City
details.content: In a city defined by masks and performance, the Sodality is a significant cultural institution. Their role in the Autumn Masquerade is central to Venturia's most important annual event.

details.label: PC Connection
details.content: Car is an apprentice here. The Sodality connection gives the party access to theater networks, Amaranth Theater relationships, and knowledge of masquerade traditions and history.

connections.relationship: Car is an apprentice; rejected her family's Archive business to join
Closely aligned; Sodality members work with the theater on major productions

factions.dm_notes: The Sodality knows things about masquerade tradition that could be investigative resources — the history of the Autumn Masquerade, specific rules and rituals, the significance of specific mask types. Car's access here is valuable.

factions.name: The Fog Wardens
factions.summary: A small, specialized corps that volunteers to maintain the barriers at Vallombrosa's fog line, record those who approach, and document unusual events. Considered either prestigious or disturbing depending on who you ask.

details.label: Duties
details.content: Maintain barriers at the fog boundary, record names of anyone approaching the fog line, document unusual phenomena, and keep the garrison logs that go back generations.

details.label: Effects of Service
details.content: Most rotations last only a few months. Extended exposure produces sleep difficulties, persistent feelings of being watched, and dreams about places they've never been. Some Wardens request extended service regardless.

details.label: Disappearance Pattern
details.content: Valentro's father (an experienced Fog Warden) disappeared some time ago. Noname's fiancé Marcus (a Fog Warden) disappeared the same night Silas appeared. This pattern of Fog Wardens specifically going missing suggests either they know something that makes them targets, or their proximity to the fog makes them vulnerable to whatever is 'leaking' from Vallombrosa before Marcello's arrival.

connections.relationship: Noname is a Fog Warden; her fiancé Marcus was a Fog Warden who disappeared
Valentro's father was a Fog Warden who disappeared before the campaign
Their base of operations at the Overlook

factions.dm_notes: The garrison logs are an investigative resource. Other Wardens who were on duty the night Marcus disappeared may have partial memories or may have had their memories affected. The pattern of experienced Wardens disappearing specifically is a major clue about the campaign's early mystery.

factions.name: The Covenant Archive
factions.summary: An institution maintaining records of binding agreements, contracts, and promises throughout Venturia. Run by Car's parents. Distinct from — but related in theme to — the Covenant of Silver Scales merchant association.

details.label: Function
details.content: Maintains records of agreements and contracts throughout Venturia. Car's family has run this institution for generations. Car rejected it as her family path.

details.label: PC Access
details.content: Car's family connection gives her (and through her, the party) access to contract records and Archive resources. Roxy cultivated friendship with Car partly for this access.

connections.relationship: Her parents run it; she rejected it as her path

factions.dm_notes: May hold records relevant to investigation — if suspicious deals or contracts can be traced through institutional records. The covenant theme connects to the campaign's broader exploration of binding oaths, fey contracts, and the Keeper of Faces' domain.

factions.name: The Covenant of Silver Scales
factions.summary: Venturia's premier merchant association specializing in high-value commercial agreements and contract law. Led by Vincenzo Delcourt, who has discovered that certain contracts made in specific city locations carry unexpected supernatural weight.

details.label: Function
details.content: Expert legal counsel for commercial matters, mediation services, connections with wealthy merchants throughout the region, and priority access to lucrative business opportunities. Covenant membership signals that you honor agreements.

details.label: Leader
details.content: Vincenzo Delcourt: mid-forties, old money, self-made fortune through shrewd investment. Impeccably dressed, unfailingly polite, absolutely ruthless about the letter of agreements. Studies contract magic and binding oaths as a personal hobby.

details.label: Supernatural Discovery
details.content: Vincenzo has documented cases where contracts made in specific city locations — particularly anywhere within sight of Vallombrosa — become magically binding without intentional spellcasting. The Covenant has been exploiting this to ensure important contracts are negotiated in high-binding locations without telling clients.

details.label: Registry of Broken Oaths
details.content: A confidential record of every significant contract breach in Venturia going back over a century. Contains embarrassing secrets about many powerful people. Vincenzo has noticed the rate of supernatural consequences for oath-breaking has increased over the past fifty years — suggesting the magical influence is getting stronger.

details.label: Di Errante Discovery
details.content: The Covenant has found fragmentary evidence that the di Errante family, immediately before their tragedy, was negotiating some massive contract or agreement. References to 'terms being finalized' and 'significant considerations changing hands.' The actual agreement has been lost or destroyed. Vincenzo is obsessed with finding it.

factions.dm_notes: The Covenant's supernatural binding discovery, the Registry of Broken Oaths, and the di Errante contract fragments are all investigative goldmines. The di Errante pre-tragedy agreement could be Lorenzo's bargain with the Keeper formalized in some mortal legal instrument — finding it could reveal crucial information. The increasing oath-consequence rate maps to the prison weakening.

factions.name: The City Watch and Shadowed Wardens
factions.summary: Venturia's law enforcement, organized as a public force and a secretive division called the Shadowed Wardens who handle supernatural threats. Commanded by Captain Alessia Marren.

details.label: Structure
details.content: Visible public force patrols streets and responds to immediate problems. The Shadowed Wardens are a much smaller secretive division handling supernatural threats. Most citizens only know about the regular Watch.

details.label: Captain Alessia Marren
details.content: Late thirties, rose through ranks through competence, political savvy, and willingness to make hard decisions. Tough, fair-minded, deeply protective of Venturia's citizens. Backs her people against political pressure. Carries the weight of knowing things about Venturia's supernatural nature that keep her up at night.

details.label: Supernatural Records
details.content: The Shadowed Wardens have maintained secret records of supernatural incidents for nearly two hundred years. These records document phenomena clustering around certain locations and times, particularly the Autumn Masquerade and Vallombrosa's boundary. They've tracked every disappearance into the fog and maintained files on each person.

details.label: Containment Protocols
details.content: Procedures for dealing with supernatural threats without public panic — methods for discretely handling supernatural creatures, techniques for memory alteration or selective information suppression, and contingency plans for various catastrophic scenarios. Alessia is deeply uncomfortable with some protocols, particularly memory manipulation, but understands why they exist.

details.label: The Registry
details.content: The Wardens maintain a confidential registry of individuals throughout Venturia displaying supernatural abilities or unusual connections to the city's fey-touched nature. Ostensibly for protection and monitoring, but Alessia recognizes how easily such a list could be weaponized. She is trying to reform this practice.

factions.dm_notes: The Shadowed Wardens' two-hundred-year record archive is an enormous investigative resource if the party can access it. Alessia is a potential ally — she'll be looking for capable individuals to help with threats the Watch can't handle alone. The Registry of supernatural individuals may include PCs, which creates tension. Her discomfort with memory manipulation protocols aligns her philosophically with the party's investigation.

factions.name: The Harbor Wardens
factions.summary: The organization overseeing maritime trade and port security in Venturia's harbor. Works closely with the Harbor-Lords on the Autumn Council.

details.label: Function
details.content: Maintains port security, oversees maritime trade, and coordinates with the Harbor-Lords who represent their interests on the Autumn Council.

factions.dm_notes: Relatively minor faction but important for harbor-related investigation threads. Their records of unusual ships or cargo could be investigative resources.

government.name: The Autumn Council
government.summary: Venturia's ruling body of eleven voting members split among Harbor-Lords, Guild-Masters, Temple Authority, and the Watch-Captain. Chronically deadlocked at 3-3-3-1, achieving little during the current crisis.

details.label: Structure
details.content: Eleven voting members: three Harbor-Lords, three Guild-Masters, three Temple Authority representatives, one Watch-Captain, and one rotating Council Chair (currently Temple Authority). Meets weekly in Council Hall, High Quarter.

details.label: Voting Requirements
details.content: Simple majority (6 votes) for routine business. Two-thirds majority (8 votes) for emergency powers, significant taxation, or entering the fog zone. Unanimous vote to overturn founding charter laws.

details.label: The Gridlock Problem
details.content: Each faction bloc-votes on most issues, creating 3-3-3-1 deadlock. The Watch-Captain and Chair become critical swing votes, meaning almost nothing gets done without extensive negotiation. During crisis, this is catastrophic.

details.label: Marcello's Exploitation Strategy
details.content: Marcello doesn't need to control the council — the gridlock already paralyzes it. He charms Harbor-Lords with economic solutions, exploits Guild-Master divisions, manipulates Temple Authority through false friendship, and makes a deal with the Watch-Captain. By the time they recognize the danger, he is already established.

government.dm_notes: The gridlock is a feature for party engagement — they can navigate the political maze, offer coalition-building help, or exploit divisions to get authorization for things they need. The council can't effectively resist Marcello once he arrives. Individual council members approaching the party privately for help or information are good hooks.

government.name: Lord-Admiral Teodor Vast
government.summary: The wealthiest Harbor-Lord on the Council, commanding the largest private merchant fleet in Venturia. Blunt, pragmatic, and impatient with philosophy. Believes transactional solutions exist for most problems.

details.label: Background
details.content: Rose from ship's captain to fleet owner through skill, ruthlessness, and strategic marriages. Controls the largest private merchant fleet based in Venturia. Affects a 'simple sailor' persona when convenient.

details.label: Council Position
details.content: Blunt and pragmatic. 'Pay the party to solve it, pay VAVA to research it — but solve it and let us get back to business.' Opposed the initial hiring of the party but now that supernatural elements are undeniable, wants decisive action.

details.label: Exploitable Weakness
details.content: His fleet captains are reporting increasingly strange phenomena at sea — time distortions, crew members with false memories, compasses behaving erratically near the island. He's terrified this will spread beyond Venturia and destroy his entire commercial network. Will pay almost anything to prevent that.

government.dm_notes: His fleet's supernatural phenomena (time distortions, false memories at sea) are evidence of the prison's weakening effects spreading. He's a potential financial patron for the party if they can demonstrate they'll solve the problem.

government.name: Constanza Marea
government.summary: A sharp, ambitious Harbor-Lord representing the Warehouse Consortium. Newer to the Council but compensates with aggressive political instincts. Sees the crisis as an opportunity if she plays it right.

details.label: Background
details.content: Represents the Warehouse Consortium. Family has run the largest warehouse complex in the Harbor District for four generations. Newer to the council (three years). Excellent at reading rooms and finding leverage points.

details.label: Council Position
details.content: 'Everyone wants power and money. Fine. Let's trade. What does everyone need to feel safe enough to let commerce resume? Let's broker that deal.'

details.label: Secret Undertow Connection
details.content: She has been meeting secretly with The Undertow's representatives. She either owes them something significant or is working a deal that would horrify the rest of the council. The party could discover this and gain leverage — or become targets if they threaten to expose it.

connections.relationship: Secret meetings with Undertow representatives; nature of obligation or deal unknown

government.dm_notes: Her Undertow connection is leverage or a threat to the party. What exactly does she owe The Undertow, or what deal is she working? This could tie into the broader memory manipulation network. She's the most politically sophisticated Harbor-Lord — could be an ally or antagonist depending on party approach.

government.name: Vincen Tidecaller
government.summary: Master of the Tidekeeper's Lodge and Harbor-Lord by ancient charter right. Quiet and thoughtful, speaks rarely but commands attention when he does. Carries generations of knowledge about Venturia's waters — including things he isn't sharing.

details.label: Background
details.content: Master of the Tidekeeper's Lodge. Not wealthy like other Harbor-Lords — influence comes from the Lodge's essential function and accumulated knowledge. Was the compromise candidate when Teodor and Constanza deadlocked two years ago.

details.label: Council Position
details.content: 'The waters have been strange for months. The fog moves wrong. Something's waking up that was sleeping. This isn't a problem you throw money at — it's older than your warehouses and your trade routes. We need to understand it before we act.'

details.label: Hidden Knowledge
details.content: He knows something specific about Vallombrosa's history that he's not sharing — something the Tidekeepers recorded centuries ago. The party could earn his trust and learn it, or discover he's been withholding information that could have saved lives.

government.dm_notes: The Tidekeepers' pre-curse records are a major investigative resource. What specifically does Vincen know? Earning his trust should be a meaningful process — he's traditional and suspicious of rapid change, but also genuinely cares about understanding before acting.

government.name: Master Artisan Celeste Orion
government.summary: Guild-Master of the Binding Crafts Guild and passionate advocate for Venturia's working people. Daughter of a Liminal House foundling, she built her career from nothing and is fiercely protective of workers' rights.

details.label: Background
details.content: Master of the Binding Crafts Guild (carpenters, masons, shipwrights, construction trades). Built reputation on major architectural projects. Daughter of a Liminal House foundling — built her career from nothing.

details.label: Council Position
details.content: 'My people are the ones disappearing. My people are the ones The Bound are recruited from. We need protection NOW — more Watch patrols in working districts, hazard pay for anyone working near the fog line, immediate compensation for families of the disappeared.'

details.label: Personal Crisis
details.content: Her eldest son vanished three weeks ago. Officially she's recused from cases involving the disappeared, but she's privately desperate and might make irrational decisions or trust the wrong people if they promise information about him.

details.label: Liminal House Connection
details.content: Her being the daughter of a Liminal House foundling means she has personal family connection to the fog child phenomenon. Her father was a fog child — like Noname's father. This could give her personal motivation to help the party investigate Liminal House's secrets.

government.dm_notes: Her missing son is powerful leverage for her as an ally — she'll work with the party if they promise to look for him. Her Liminal House connection through her father is a potential personal stake in the fog child investigation. She's the most emotionally accessible council member to the party.

government.name: Watch-Captain Thora Iron
government.summary: The Watch-Captain on the Autumn Council, commanding both the public Watch and the secret Shadowed Wardens. A pragmatist willing to do what is necessary to protect the city, including things the council would never authorize.

details.label: Background
details.content: Technically the Watch-Captain is Alessia Marren who commands the Watch. The Watch-Captain council seat is occupied by someone who must navigate between the council factions alone, making them a critical but politically isolated swing vote.

details.label: Off-Books Operations
details.content: The Watch-Captain has been conducting operations without council authorization — illegal detentions, aggressive interrogations, surveillance violating Venturian law. If exposed, this could destroy her politically. Alternatively, the party could leverage this to get unofficial help with things the council won't authorize.

details.label: Marcello's Target
details.content: Marcello will offer the Watch-Captain power she's been unable to get from the council: 'I can identify the Bound before they activate. I can prevent disasters before they happen. Work with me, and you'll finally be able to protect this city the way you know it needs protection.' She may accept even if suspicious of his motives.

government.dm_notes: The Watch-Captain is a double-edged ally — willing to do things the council won't authorize (useful to the party) but also potentially compromised by Marcello's offer. Her off-books operations could be leverage for the party or a threat. She's the most politically isolated council member but also the most pragmatic.

history.name: The di Errante Tragedy
history.summary: The catastrophic event approximately 1,100 years ago in which the di Errante family's destruction — rooted in jealousy, a supernatural bargain, and a stolen identity — transformed the family's vast estate into the cursed fog-valley of Vallombrosa.

details.label: What Is Known Publicly
details.content: The di Errante estates on the western coast became Vallombrosa — a fog-shrouded valley that has never cleared. The di Errante family was destroyed. Legends about the cause vary widely. No one publicly knows the specific truth.

details.label: The Brothers
details.content: Marcello di Errante was the elder brother — powerful, accomplished, genuinely beloved, with fey-touched nature and shadow manipulation abilities. Lorenzo was the younger brother, driven by jealousy and entitlement, unable to accept that Isabella chose his brother freely.

details.label: Isabella Fortuna
details.content: A woman of considerable beauty who possessed genuine prophetic abilities. She loved Marcello freely and completely. She never chose Lorenzo of her own will.

details.label: Lorenzo's Bargain
details.content: Seven years before the wedding, Lorenzo sought out the Keeper of Faces and negotiated a bargain: supernatural power to coerce Isabella's love in exchange for 'the face you show the world.' He believed the price was metaphorical. The actual price was his literal identity, name, and sense of self — collected on the anniversary, making him appear as Marcello and unable to speak his own name.

details.label: The Sphinx Consultation
details.content: Shortly after making his bargain, Lorenzo consulted Tartuzi the sphinx, who told him the exact collection date and provided the basic principles for binding an Archfey. This consultation, given freely according to sphinx nature, led directly to Tartuzi's own millennia of enslavement.

details.label: The Wedding Day
details.content: Isabella hired an adventuring party (The Emerald Tide party) to prevent the Keeper from collecting the debt. What she didn't know: Lorenzo had orchestrated their arrival using her own prophetic abilities against her. The party performed a binding ritual using a mirror, compass, and hourglass — trapping the Keeper at the moment of legitimate debt collection.

details.label: The Cosmic Theft
details.content: With the Keeper trapped and helpless, Lorenzo drained power directly from the imprisoned Archfey, transforming himself into something far more than human. This collapsed the magical coercion sustaining Isabella's false love. She confronted Lorenzo in horror. When she could no longer love him, he murdered her with his stolen power.

details.label: The Final Battle
details.content: Marcello attacked Lorenzo in grief and rage. The brothers fought an evenly matched battle that destroyed the ceremonial gardens. Lorenzo killed Marcello. Tartuzi appeared — pulled by the completed binding mechanism to become the eternal lock of the prison. The adventuring party was knocked unconscious, imprisoned in the estate dungeon, and offered poisoned wine. Their ultimate fate is unknown.

details.label: The Curse
details.content: The combination of the Keeper's imprisonment, the stolen power, and the murders transformed the entire di Errante estate into Vallombrosa — a cursed fog-valley from which people emerge as fog children with no memories, where time moves strangely, and where the imprisoned supernatural forces still exert influence on the surrounding area.

connections.relationship: Was imprisoned during this event; remains imprisoned in Vallombrosa today
Became the eternal lock of the Keeper's prison during this event; has been trapped for a thousand years
The arriving Marcello is either the original Marcello or Lorenzo wearing his stolen identity — it remains ambiguous

history.dm_notes: Players begin knowing distorted legend versions of this event. Discovering the true history is a major campaign-long investigative arc. The adventuring party (Emerald Tide) may be connected to PC reincarnations. The di Errante bargain with the Covenant (fragmentary records exist) may be relevant to understanding the full scope of what Lorenzo negotiated.

history.name: Vallombrosa
history.summary: The fog-shrouded valley covering the old di Errante estate, created by the ancient tragedy and now the source of fog children, supernatural phenomena, and the imprisoned forces that increasingly affect all of Venturia.

details.label: What Venturia Knows
details.content: Vallombrosa has existed for over a thousand years as a fog-shrouded valley where people occasionally emerge with no memories. Legends about what it is vary. Most citizens grew up with it as part of their landscape — spooky and mysterious but just there. Nobody seriously believes any specific version of the legends as historical fact.

details.label: The Fog Line
details.content: The boundary between Venturia and Vallombrosa is maintained by the Fog Wardens. The fog line has measurably moved over the years — sometimes closer to the city, sometimes farther — by inches or feet. The movement appears to be accelerating.

details.label: Strange Phenomena Near the Boundary
details.content: Lights moving in the mist that couldn't be torches. Voices in unrecognized languages. Figures visible just inside the boundary, standing motionless before fading. Extended exposure produces sleep difficulties, persistent feelings of being watched, and dreams about places the dreamer has never been.

details.label: What Vallombrosa Actually Is
details.content: The di Errante family estate, transformed by the imprisonment of the Keeper of Faces and the theft of its power. The fog is a manifestation of the supernatural forces contained within. The fog children who emerge are souls cycling through the liminal space — some were people who wandered in and emerged unchanged while decades passed outside. Others may be born within Vallombrosa.

details.label: The Prison Weakening
details.content: The Keeper's prison is weakening. Evidence: the fog line's accelerating movement, Silas's appearance, the increasing rate of supernatural oath-consequences, and the phenomena Teodor Vast's ships are experiencing at sea. The weakening will reach crisis point when Marcello arrives.

details.label: The Three Fanes
details.content: Three supernatural anchors within Vallombrosa maintain the imprisonment mechanism.
